These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel to copy from.
In this task, different layers exhibit different specialties: some layers at- tend to the pixels that are to be copied, and some layers attend to the target lines alone the eight directions.
This visualization suggests that our method attempts to learn the relations between different tasks, which is an essential ability for abstraction and reasoning.

Although most ARC tasks are unambiguous, some may admit multiple plausible explanations or rules.
Layers at different depths tend to focus on different structures. Early layers tend to focus on local transformations and context.
Middle layers tend to perform a more non-local connection, e.g., horizontally or vertically.
The deep layers are more task-specialized.
